title: [blastoise] Battle Guide
summary: A balanced 79/100/105 [water] special tank that splits its workload between [rapid-spin] hazard control and clean [ice-beam] coverage.
strengths: 79/100/105 bulk lets it take a hit and return fire reliably
[rapid-spin] combines hazard removal with a Speed boost
weaknesses: Grass and Electric 2x weaknesses are brutal
Speed 78 means it usually moves second against attackers
Lacks finishing power until [torrent] triggers below 1/3 HP

recommendedMoves.reason: Main [water] STAB with the highest single-turn damage
Mandatory coverage for Grass, Dragon, and Flying
Fighting coverage for Dark and Steel walls
Hazard removal plus a free Speed boost in one slot

recommendedItems.reason: One-shot recovery for high-priority early turns
Alternative item to patch the Speed tier

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[blastoise] is a balanced [water]-type special tank that stacks 79/100/105 bulk with clean coverage and [rapid-spin] utility. 85 SpA and 78 Speed look underwhelming, but the [hydro-pump] / [ice-beam] / [aura-sphere] line hits most of the metagame from one slot while doubling as a hazard remover.

## Strengths
HP-invested 100/105 mixed bulk lets [blastoise] eat a neutral special hit from nearly any top attacker and return fire. [modest] plus [leftovers] keeps 85 SpA relevant as chip damage, and the STAB + coverage trio covers Grass, Dragon, Dark, and Steel angles that most Waters miss. [rapid-spin] clears stealth-rock while giving a Speed boost, turning a utility turn into a setup turn.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Grass and Electric hit for 2x and gate [blastoise] from switching into any competent attacker on those types. Speed 78 is below most unboosted threats, so the Tera call often gets forced early. [torrent] only activates below 1/3 HP, so the damage floor is thin against walls — you must force trades with [ice-beam] or [aura-sphere] before opponents stabilize behind recovery.

## Recommended Moveset
[hydro-pump] is the locked STAB because the raw damage keeps [blastoise] offensively relevant. [ice-beam] is mandatory coverage for Grass, Dragon, and Flying. [aura-sphere] punishes Dark and Steel walls that pivot in expecting a free turn. The last slot is [rapid-spin] for hazard control and the Speed boost — flash-cannon or dark-pulse are viable swaps if the team does not need a spinner.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default item for the long-game plan. [sitrus-berry] is the alternative when one guaranteed heal matters. [choice-scarf] is the offensive variant that turns [blastoise] into a revenge killer. Keep [torrent] for the low-HP damage spike. Tera [water] preserves STAB, Tera [fairy] flips Grass, Dark, and Dragon weaknesses at once.

## Teammates & Synergy
[blastoise] is the special wall and hazard remover, so pair it with a physical wall on the Steel or Ground axis. A Ground-type partner covers Electric, and volt-switch or u-turn pivots bring [blastoise] in at the right turn. dragon-dance and nasty-plot sweepers behind it benefit directly from the cleared field.

## Play Patterns
Bring [blastoise] in on turn two or three — let a pivot eat the early chip, then [rapid-spin] on a predicted switch. Save Tera for a telegraphed Grass or Electric move, and aim [hydro-pump] in [torrent] range when a game-winning KO is on the line.
